author: y4rr
content:
Phase 2B complete. Added feature-gated Arrow / Polars interop in ferrotorch-data/src/interop.rs (~360 LOC, 15 tests).

Cargo features in ferrotorch-data:
- 'arrow' (default off): pulls in ferray-numpy-interop and arrow=58, enables tensor<->Arrow PrimitiveArray + RecordBatch helpers
- 'polars' (default off, implies 'arrow'): adds polars=0.46 + dataframe_to_tensor

Functions:
- tensor_to_arrow_array<T>(t) -> PrimitiveArray<T::ArrowType> — single contiguous buffer, no shape preserved
- tensor_from_arrow_array<T>(arr, shape) -> Tensor<T> — explicit shape on the way back; rejects nulls
- record_batch_to_tensor<T>(rb) -> Tensor<T> — homogeneous numeric RecordBatch -> [n_rows, n_cols] row-major
- tensor_to_arrow_arrayref<T>(t) -> ArrayRef — dyn-dispatch helper
- dataframe_to_tensor<T>(df) -> Tensor<T> [polars feature only] — Polars DataFrame, casts columns via Polars before extraction

Type bound:  — works for f32 and f64 (the intersection of ferrotorch's Float trait and Arrow's primitive types). bf16 isn't an Arrow native type, so it's excluded by the trait bound; users must convert to f32/f64 first if they want Arrow interop on bf16 data.

Implementation notes:
- Skipped ferray-numpy-interop's arrayd_to_arrow_flat / arrayd_from_arrow_flat round-trip in favor of constructing the Arrow PrimitiveArray directly from the tensor data via Buffer::from_vec(data). ferray-numpy-interop's value-add was the dtype-mapping layer, but we know T statically.
- dataframe_to_tensor extracts via Polars' AnyValue::try_extract::<f64>() iteration with a final T::from cast — robust against numeric source dtypes and avoids navigating Polars' NumericNative trait jungle.
- One small fix in ferrotorch-data/src/dataset.rs: an existing test used  which became ambiguous when serde_json's  joined the dependency graph (transitive via polars). Made the empty-slice literal explicit .
- Pinned arrow=58 and polars=0.46 to match ferray-numpy-interop 0.3.1's transitive deps and avoid duplicate-arrow resolution in the dep graph.

GPU discipline (apply /rust-gpu-discipline):
- Module docstring documents Arrow/Polars are CPU formats; conversions produce CPU-resident Tensor<T>; user moves to GPU explicitly with .to(Device::Cuda(_))
- tensor_to_arrow_array calls is_cuda() at entry; CUDA tensors return NotImplementedOnCuda — never silently downloaded
- tensor_from_arrow_array always returns CPU tensors regardless of where the user puts them next
- Forbidden patterns grep clean: no automatic .to(Device::Cuda) inside conversion fns

Tests (15 total):
- 11 in arrow path: round-trip f32/f64, 2-D preservation via explicit shape, shape-mismatch rejection, null-rejection, ArrayRef dyn-cast, RecordBatch matrix assembly, zero-columns rejection, dtype-mismatch rejection, null-column rejection, CPU-tensor accepted
- 4 in polars path: f64 basic, f32-with-cast (i64 source), null rejection, empty-DataFrame rejection

Default-feature workspace: 4566 passed, 0 failed (no regression from adding the feature-gated module). Closes #570 (Phase 2B).
